jokes.text: OH, NO. IT'S NOT... IT'S NOT A "CHRISTIAN MUSIC" BONFIRE. IT'S A CHRISTIAN "MUSIC BONFIRE." YOU KNOW, WE BURN ALL SATANIC MUSIC.
jokes.setup: Michael assumes it's a bonfire featuring Christian music
jokes.punchline: George Michael corrects him - they're burning non-Christian music, not celebrating Christian music
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay misdirection with perfect economy. The quotation mark emphasis creates the joke structure, and it fits George Michael's earnest character perfectly.

jokes.text: THE THING IS, THOUGH, THE ONLY MUSIC I HAVE IS EITHER INSTRUCTIONAL OR HUMOR.
jokes.setup: George Michael needs music to burn
jokes.punchline: His music collection is pathetically limited to instructional and comedy records
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - shows George Michael's sheltered, innocent nature through his hilariously limited music taste.

jokes.text: HEY, GEORGE MICHAEL. JESUS CALLED. HE WANTS YOUR THRILLER ALBUM.
jokes.setup: George Michael is worried about the Christian bonfire
jokes.punchline: Gob sarcastically suggests even Jesus wants Michael Jackson's Thriller burned
jokes.explanation: Sharp, quotable line with great economy. The absurdity of Jesus personally requesting a specific album fits Gob's irreverent character.

jokes.text: IT'S THE ONLY WAY WE'RE EVER GONNA GET IT AIR-CONDITIONED.
jokes.setup: Gob offers to work at the banana stand
jokes.punchline: George Michael reveals the family's financial desperation - they need Gob's incompetence to justify air conditioning
jokes.explanation: Clever setup-punchline that reveals family dysfunction and financial desperation through practical logic.

jokes.text: OKAY. NOW I'D LIKE TO USE UP MY FAVOR AND DECLINE.
jokes.setup: Lucille asks how Michael would like her to owe him a favor
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately uses the theoretical favor to decline her actual request
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure with excellent economy. Michael's quick thinking and the logical absurdity make it quotable.

jokes.text: When Buster inadvertently bid on Lucille 2. - $10,000! - Buster.
jokes.setup: Lucille explains why Buster can't bid on her
jokes.punchline: Flashback shows Buster enthusiastically bidding on the wrong woman
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Buster's bumbling nature and complicated family dynamics.

jokes.text: REALLY? DID NOTHING CANCEL?
jokes.setup: Gob says he has the afternoon free
jokes.punchline: Lucille's deadpan surprise that Gob isn't busy, revealing her low opinion of him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - her casual cruelty delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: I STOPPED BY. I HADN'T CHOSEN A SIDE.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Lindsay was protesting hunters
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reveals she attended a protest without knowing which side she was on
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lindsay's vapid, unfocused activism. The casual admission of complete ignorance is very funny.

jokes.text: YOU'RE NOT EVEN ALLOWED TO FIRE YOUR GUN IN THE AIR... IS THAT FRANK WRENCH?
jokes.setup: Lindsay is listing gun regulations seriously
jokes.punchline: She gets completely distracted mid-sentence by spotting a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Lindsay's shallow priorities and short attention span.

jokes.text: WELL, THAT'S ONE RULE I'M NOT GONNA FOLLOW.
jokes.setup: Moses Taylor plays a rule-abiding detective
jokes.punchline: He immediately breaks character to dismiss the Second Amendment
jokes.explanation: Simple irony joke about the actor contradicting his TV character. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: AND I'M GONNA SEE IF I CAN GET A WRENCH TO STRIP MY NUTS.
jokes.setup: Lindsay is talking about her date with Moses Taylor
jokes.punchline: Double entendre using his character name 'Frank Wrench' in a sexual context
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual innuendo that's more cringe than clever. The follow-up admission makes it slightly better.

jokes.text: I WAS TRYING TO BE SEXY. IT JUST GOT AWAY FROM ME.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's awkward sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: She admits the line failed and explains her intention
jokes.explanation: Good recovery that acknowledges the failed innuendo and makes it funnier through self-awareness.

jokes.text: SEEMS THEY DON'T APPRECIATE COMPETITION.
jokes.setup: Tobias got a cease and desist from Blue Man Group
jokes.punchline: He frames being legally threatened as professional rivalry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's delusional self-importance and inability to read situations.

jokes.text: JESUS! IT'S THE WOLF! THAT IS THE WOLF! THE WOLF IS UPSTAIRS!
jokes.setup: Talk about proving masculinity
jokes.punchline: Tobias panics at children's record sound effects
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction of Tobias's masculinity claims. His terror at a children's record undermines everything he just said.

jokes.text: I THOUGHT THAT WAS A HOMEFILL.
jokes.setup: Michael explains it's Peter and the Wolf record
jokes.punchline: Tobias uses 'homefill' instead of 'home decoration' or similar word
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias malapropism. His tendency to invent words fits his pretentious character.

jokes.text: YES, THERE IS A WOLF ON THE LOOSE. HIS NAME IS OSCAR.
jokes.setup: George Sr. is asked about the wolf reports around town
jokes.punchline: He reveals he's the 'wolf' and he's talking about his brother Oscar
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that reframes the wolf reports as George Sr.'s jealousy calls about Oscar dating Lucille.

jokes.text: HOPE THAT WAS FORCED.
jokes.setup: George Sr. mentions he hasn't had a massage since prison
jokes.punchline: Michael hopes his father wasn't willingly intimate with other inmates
jokes.explanation: Dark prison humor delivered with Michael's characteristic deadpan discomfort about his father's situation.

jokes.text: AND THOSE, UH, PREMADE BOIL-IN-BAG MEALS... YOU JUST TOSS THOSE IN THERE.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explains hot tub benefits
jokes.punchline: He wants to cook frozen dinners in the hot tub water he'll also bathe in
jokes.explanation: Gross and absurd plan that shows George Sr.'s complete disconnection from normal hygiene standards.

jokes.text: IT LOOKS LIKE MY BOY IS GONNA BE STICKING IT TO YOUR DAUGHTER, SITWELL.
jokes.setup: Flashback to young Michael and Sally
jokes.punchline: George Sr. uses crude sexual language about his young son and Sally
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag of inappropriate sexual competition between the fathers that affects Michael.

jokes.text: NO, WE'RE NOT GONNA BE STICKING ANYTHING. NOT-NOT GONNA STICK A THING.
jokes.setup: Sally mentions going or sticking around
jokes.punchline: Michael overreacts to innocent use of 'stick', creating awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Shows how his father's inappropriate comments traumatized Michael, making him overreact to innocent words.

jokes.text: IT'S LIGHTER FLUID. DIDN'T PUT IN A NEW FLINT.
jokes.setup: Gob does a magic trick with fire
jokes.punchline: His lighter malfunctions and sprays Sally with lighter fluid instead of producing flame
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob magic failure. His incompetence creates an awkward and potentially dangerous situation.

jokes.text: SMOOTH, MICHAEL, LIKE HER FATHER'S HEAD, CHEST, ARMS, LEGS AND ASS.
jokes.setup: Gob calls Michael smooth sarcastically
jokes.punchline: Reveals Sally's father is completely hairless in an overly detailed way
jokes.explanation: Good escalating description that builds the absurdity through anatomical detail. The unexpected information is funny.

jokes.text: JUST SO YOU CAN SHOW UP AND SAY, 'CAR TROUBLES?'
jokes.setup: Michael describes Gob's predatory dating tactics
jokes.punchline: The overly smooth, predictable line Gob would use
jokes.explanation: Sets up a running gag and perfectly captures Gob's sleazy confidence. Very quotable line that will pay off later.

jokes.text: LUPE WASHED THEM, AND THEY SPROUTED. I THREW THEM OUT.
jokes.setup: Oscar looking for his other pair of pants
jokes.punchline: His pants literally grew like plants when washed
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual that suggests Oscar's pants are made of organic material. Perfectly economical and surreal.

jokes.text: I PUT UP ONE TO GET IT. AS LONG AS IT GETS FRANK WRENCH TO BID ON ME.
jokes.setup: Lindsay talking about her fur coat acquisition
jokes.punchline: She fought an animal to get the fur and wants to use it to attract a man
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Lindsay's hypocrisy - fighting animals for fashion after protesting hunting.

jokes.text: SHOUT OUT, 'I GET HER 364 DAYS A YEAR FOR FREE!' OR SOMETHING.
jokes.setup: Michael agrees to bid on Lucille at auction
jokes.punchline: Lucille suggests a cruel exit line about their mother-son relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel Lucille line that reduces their relationship to a business transaction. Very quotable dark humor.

jokes.text: THEY'RE LITERALLY BLUE, NOT SAD.
jokes.setup: Tobias sees a Blue Man Group audition flyer
jokes.punchline: He clarifies the literal blueness vs. emotional state
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that shows Tobias's literal-minded thinking and explains his Blue Man Group obsession.

jokes.text: I CAN'T WEAR THIS. I LOOK LIKE I'M 16.
jokes.setup: Michael gets a country club outfit
jokes.punchline: The preppy clothes make the adult Michael look juvenile
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about country club dress code making Michael look childish when trying to impress Sally.

jokes.text: I DIDN'T EVEN WANNA HAVE LUNCH WITH HER IN THE FIRST PLACE.
jokes.setup: Gob is caught having lunch with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: He protests too much about not wanting to be there
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's embarrassment about his relationship with Lucille 2. His overprotesting reveals his feelings.

jokes.text: PROBABLY FORGOT HER OLD LADY GLASSES.
jokes.setup: Gob is reading the menu to Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: He makes a cruel age-related comment about her eyesight
jokes.explanation: Cruel but character-appropriate line showing Gob's inability to be genuinely romantic even when trying.

jokes.text: I OVERDID IT.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what George Sr. did to max out the credit card
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement while sitting in a hot tub disaster in an attic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement. Two words that completely fail to capture the absurd disaster he's created.

jokes.text: I TRIED TO DRINK SOME OF THE WATER, AND IT WAS TOO HOT, AND IT TASTED LIKE SOY SAUCE.
jokes.setup: George Sr. installed a hot tub in the attic
jokes.punchline: He tried to drink the cooking/bathing water and got burned
jokes.explanation: Gross and absurd consequence of his terrible hot tub cooking plan. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: DID YOU STICK IT TO HER?
jokes.setup: George Sr. asks about Michael's encounter with Sally
jokes.punchline: Uses the same crude language that traumatized Michael as a child
jokes.explanation: Callback to the childhood trauma, showing George Sr. hasn't learned anything and still talks this way.

jokes.text: Is that veal marsala stuck in the intake?
jokes.setup: Hot tub disasters continuing
jokes.punchline: Fancy frozen dinner clogging the hot tub machinery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cooking disaster with specific food item adding to the absurdity.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW SOMETHING? THE REASON I NEVER WENT AFTER HER IS BECAUSE YOU'RE ALWAYS PUSHING ME.
jokes.setup: George Sr. continues his crude comments about Sally
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes he's been rebelling against his father's pushing by not pursuing her
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Michael's psychology and his complicated relationship with his father's influence.

jokes.text: BUT, YOU KNOW, I DON'T KNOW. SOMEBODY'S GOTTA BURN, RIGHT?
jokes.setup: George Michael can't decide what music to burn
jokes.punchline: He decides someone has to be sacrificed regardless of guilt
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing George Michael's innocent acceptance of illogical persecution. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: I GOT SOME LOOKS ON THE BUS 'CAUSE OF THIS.
jokes.setup: Tobias enters with a large tranquilizer gun
jokes.punchline: He rode public transportation while carrying what looks like a weapon
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy imagining Tobias casually carrying a large gun on public transport, oblivious to how threatening he looks.

jokes.text: What timing, huh? I mean, right out back they were having one.
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning the gun show loophole
jokes.punchline: Conveniently there was a gun show happening right there
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist coincidence showing how easy it is to circumvent gun laws in the show's universe.

jokes.text: CAR TROUBLES?
jokes.setup: Gob sabotages Sally's car and shows up to help
jokes.punchline: Uses the exact smooth line Michael predicted he would say
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff. Gob says exactly what Michael predicted, confirming his predictable sleaziness. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: I'M BEING SAVED BY FRANK WRENCH.
jokes.setup: Lindsay falls and Moses Taylor runs away
jokes.punchline: She thinks the actor running away is heroically saving her
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Lindsay's delusion. She misreads his cowardly retreat as heroic rescue.

jokes.text: CAR TROUBLE.
jokes.setup: Sally finds Michael after Gob's plan backfired spectacularly
jokes.punchline: Michael uses Gob's smooth line after the car exploded
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that becomes ironic - the line is literally true now but delivered after disaster rather than smooth sabotage.

jokes.text: NO! I WAS ASHAMED TO BE SEEN WITH YOU. I LIKE BEING WITH YOU.
jokes.setup: Sally accuses Michael of being ashamed to be with her
jokes.punchline: Michael clarifies he likes her privately but is ashamed publicly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's honesty and awkward attempts to explain his feelings.

jokes.text: I'M SORRY, SIR. YOUR BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD WAS DENIED. WE'VE CALLED YOUR MOTHER.
jokes.setup: Tobias is injured and needs medical care
jokes.punchline: His insurance is denied and they called his mother like he's a child
jokes.explanation: Good emasculation of Tobias. After trying to prove his masculinity, he's treated like a child with no insurance.

jokes.text: YEAH, I HOPE I DIDN'T CONFUSE PEOPLE BY SAYING IT WAS A CD BURNING PARTY.
jokes.setup: George Michael's Christian music bonfire attracts many people
jokes.punchline: He accidentally invited people to burn any CDs, not just non-Christian music
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing George Michael's innocent mistake in his invitation wording creating the wrong kind of party.

jokes.text: STICK A WRENCH IN ME. I'M DONE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay is being auctioned while under tranquilizer effects
jokes.punchline: She makes a sexual pun about Moses Taylor's character name while high
jokes.explanation: Sexual pun playing on both 'stick a fork in me' and Frank Wrench. Made funnier by her drugged state.

jokes.text: YOU SHOT HER IN THE ASS WITH ABOUT FOUR OUNCES OF HORSE TRANQUILIZER.
jokes.setup: Tobias calls shooting Lindsay a 'small wrong'
jokes.punchline: Michael details exactly how dangerous and wrong Tobias's action was
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that specifies the absurd danger of Tobias's 'mistake.' The clinical detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're not bidding? I was hoping you would. You were? You're a good kid. You always have been.
jokes.setup: Stan asking why Michael isn't bidding
jokes.punchline: Stan wanted Michael to bid on his daughter all along
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal showing Stan's approval undermining Michael's rebellion against his father.

jokes.text: DROP IT OFF WHEN YOU DROP MY DAUGHTER OFF TONIGHT.
jokes.setup: Michael needs money and Stan needs a car
jokes.punchline: Stan casually arranges to trade his daughter for Michael's car temporarily
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Stan's business-like approach to his daughter's dating life. Treats it like a car rental.

jokes.text: MIND IF I OPEN IT UP?
jokes.setup: Stan is driving Michael's convertible
jokes.punchline: He asks permission to open the already-open convertible top
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. Stan doesn't understand how convertibles work or is being overly polite about the obvious.

jokes.text: I'D ALMOST RATHER DATE SOMEONE MY FATHER DOESN'T APPROVE OF.
jokes.setup: Sally discovers her father approves of Michael
jokes.punchline: She prefers dating men her father disapproves of
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Sally's rebellious nature mirrors Michael's psychology about parental approval.

jokes.text: THAT-THAT-THAT IS LIKE ME. THAT'S EXACTLY LIKE ME!
jokes.setup: Sally says she'd rather date someone her father disapproves of
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes this describes his exact psychology and gets excited
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Michael recognizes their shared psychology about parental approval.

jokes.text: $10,000. OH, HOW GRAND.
jokes.setup: Gob bids $10,000 on Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Lucille makes a pun on 'grand' meaning both $1000 and magnificent
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that fits Lucille's character. The wordplay is obvious but delivered with her typical dryness.
